title: Shielded transaction builder: sign and serialize tx
description: Implement build_shielded_tx(spending_key, to_address: &str, amount_zatoshi: u64, memo: &[u8; 512]) -> Result<Vec<u8>> (raw tx bytes ready to broadcast). Steps: parse to_address with zcash_address, validate it is a valid Sapling or Unified Address; select notes from wallet DB sufficient to cover amount + fee (coin selection); build sapling_builder with the spend proofs; add outputs; finalize. Defensive programming: (1) check balance >= amount + fee before starting, return Err(InsufficientFunds { have, need }) not panic; (2) validate amount > dust_threshold (defined as const DUST_THRESHOLD: u64 = 1000; with doc comment citing ZIP-317); (3) memo must be exactly 512 bytes — assert, do not truncate silently; (4) return Err not panic on zcash_primitives builder errors; (5) log only amount and txid hash on success, never key material or note values. Use a subagent to read the zcash_primitives SaplingBuilder API before coding.
acceptance_criteria: Unit test: build a tx with mocked note selection, verify serialized bytes are non-empty and parseable. Test: amount > balance returns Err(InsufficientFunds). Test: amount <= dust_threshold returns Err. No real network calls in unit tests.
